title: SAM BAM and CRAM basics
summary: Inspect, explain, and convert core alignment-file formats without losing sort, header, or tag information needed downstream.
test_prompts: Explain how you would inspect and convert SAM, BAM, or CRAM files without breaking downstream compatibility.
Summarize the minimum checks you would run before trusting an alignment file for later QC or coverage work.

title: Alignment indexing
summary: Build and validate alignment-file indexes so BAM and CRAM inputs remain random-access ready for downstream analysis.
test_prompts: Plan an alignment-indexing step and explain when BAI, CSI, or CRAI matters for downstream access.
Tell me what you would verify before and after indexing a BAM or CRAM file.

title: BAM statistics and alignment QC
summary: Summarize BAM-level alignment statistics that gate downstream coverage, variant, or transcriptome analysis.
test_prompts: Summarize how you would use BAM statistics to decide whether an alignment is ready for downstream analysis.
Explain which BAM-level metrics matter most before interpreting coverage or variant results.

title: DNA short-read alignment
summary: Align DNA short reads reproducibly with explicit reference, pairing, and output-sorting assumptions.
test_prompts: Plan a DNA short-read alignment step and explain what metadata must be fixed before execution.
Tell me how you would hand off aligned reads so indexing and BAM QC can run safely next.

title: Alignment QC and coverage workflow
summary: Coordinate short-read alignment, indexing, BAM QC, and coverage handoff into an analysis-ready alignment workflow.
test_prompts: Outline an alignment-to-coverage workflow and show where sort, index, and BAM QC should stop downstream analysis.
Adapt an alignment QC workflow for a project that starts from FASTQ and ends at interval-level coverage summaries.

title: Genome-interval coverage analysis
summary: Compute and interpret genomic coverage across intervals with explicit target definitions and build-aware semantics.
test_prompts: Explain how you would run interval-level coverage analysis and keep the target definition explicit.
Summarize the checks that matter before treating coverage summaries as biologically meaningful.

title: Genome-interval arithmetic
summary: Apply overlap, subtract, merge, and related interval operations while preserving coordinate and strandedness semantics.
test_prompts: Plan an interval-arithmetic step and explain what coordinate assumptions must be stated before using the result.
Tell me how you would keep overlap or merge operations from silently changing the biological meaning of the intervals.

title: Genome-interval proximity operations
summary: Assign nearby genomic features with explicit window and nearest-feature logic rather than implicit assumptions.
test_prompts: Explain how you would assign nearby genomic features without over-claiming from simple distance alone.
Summarize the main choices behind nearest-feature or window-based genomic proximity operations.

title: Differential splicing analysis
summary: Detect and interpret differential alternative splicing using explicit event support, delta PSI, and replicate-aware reasoning.
test_prompts: Design a differential splicing analysis and explain what evidence path you would use for BAM versus abundance inputs.
Summarize the QC gates that must pass before reporting splicing changes as biology.

title: CDISC clinical-data handling
summary: Prepare CDISC-style clinical data so domains, joins, and analysis populations remain explicit and reproducible.
test_prompts: Explain how you would prepare CDISC SDTM domains for downstream clinical analysis without losing provenance.
Tell me what checks you would run before trusting joined CDISC tables for effect estimation.

title: Categorical clinical tests
summary: Run categorical association tests with sparse-data awareness and clinically interpretable contingency reporting.
test_prompts: Plan a categorical-test analysis and explain when Fisher exact or chi-square is more defensible.
Summarize what you would report alongside a categorical p-value so the clinical interpretation stays meaningful.

title: Clinical effect measures
summary: Compute and interpret clinical effect measures with explicit denominator, reference-group, and interval-estimation choices.
test_prompts: Explain how you would choose between odds ratio, risk ratio, and related effect measures for a clinical endpoint.
Tell me what must be explicit before interpreting treatment effect measures across clinical groups.

title: Clinical logistic regression
summary: Fit clinical logistic-regression models with explicit endpoint coding, adjustment strategy, and rare-event safeguards.
test_prompts: Plan a clinical logistic-regression analysis and explain what must be declared before the odds ratios are interpretable.
Summarize the failure modes that make a clinical logistic-regression result untrustworthy.

title: Clinical subgroup analysis
summary: Evaluate subgroup effects with explicit interaction logic, multiplicity caveats, and cohort-size awareness.
test_prompts: Explain how you would run and report subgroup analysis without turning exploratory slices into confirmatory claims.
Tell me what would make a subgroup result too unstable to emphasize in a clinical summary.

title: Clinical biostatistics workflow
summary: Coordinate CDISC preparation, categorical testing, effect estimation, regression, and subgroup review in one clinical-analysis workflow.
test_prompts: Outline a clinical biostatistics workflow from analysis-ready data preparation to subgroup interpretation.
Adapt a clinical-analysis workflow for a dataset that starts as SDTM domains and ends with model-based endpoint reporting.

title: Batch-aware study design
summary: Design batch structure and sample assignment so technical variation does not alias the main biological contrast.
test_prompts: Plan a batch-aware study design and explain how you would keep biology from being confounded with technical structure.
Summarize the checks you would run before approving a multi-batch experimental layout.

title: Multiple-testing control
summary: Choose and apply multiple-testing correction with explicit hypothesis-family boundaries and reporting rules.
test_prompts: Explain how you would define the hypothesis family before applying FDR or family-wise correction.
Tell me how you would keep multiple-testing control explicit across a large omics result table.

title: Biomedical model validation
summary: Validate biomedical predictive models with leakage-aware splits and explicit performance-gating rules.
test_prompts: Plan a biomedical model-validation strategy and explain how you would prevent leakage during tuning and evaluation.
Summarize the main conditions under which a cross-validation result should not be trusted.

title: Biomedical prediction explanation
summary: Explain biomedical model predictions with attribution methods that stay grounded in validated model behavior.
test_prompts: Explain how you would interpret biomedical model predictions with SHAP or LIME without overstating causality.
Tell me what validation context you would require before trusting feature-attribution outputs.

title: Biomedical model-evaluation workflow
summary: Coordinate validation and explanation of biomedical predictive models without collapsing generalization and attribution into one step.
test_prompts: Outline a biomedical model-evaluation workflow that validates first and explains only after performance is defensible.
Adapt a predictive-model workflow for an omics biomarker project that needs both leakage-aware validation and feature attribution.

title: Differential-expression result summary
summary: Summarize pre-computed differential-expression results into ranked findings, biological themes, and downstream-ready interpretation notes.
test_prompts: Explain how you would summarize a finished differential-expression table without re-running upstream statistics.
Tell me what must be present in a DE result table before you trust a narrative summary of the top findings.

title: Multi-sample QC reporting
summary: Aggregate sample-level QC outputs into a coherent report that keeps metric comparability and outlier logic explicit.
test_prompts: Plan a multi-sample QC reporting step and explain how you would keep incompatible metrics from being mixed together.
Summarize the main signals that should appear in an aggregate QC report before downstream analysis continues.

title: Notebook-based analysis reports
summary: Build parameterized notebook reports that stay executable, shareable, and tied to the underlying analysis inputs.
test_prompts: Explain how you would create a notebook-based report that stays reproducible instead of becoming a manually edited artifact.
Tell me what parameterization and execution checks matter most for a Jupyter analysis report.

title: R Markdown analysis reports
summary: Render R Markdown reports that preserve code, figures, and narrative linkage to the source analysis.
test_prompts: Plan an R Markdown reporting step and explain how you would keep the report reproducible across render environments.
Summarize the main choices that affect whether an R Markdown report is ready for sharing or publication.

title: Statistical graphics and palette design
summary: Create publication-quality statistical graphics with readable scales, defensible encodings, and accessible color choices.
test_prompts: Explain how you would design a statistical figure that is both publication-ready and quantitatively honest.
Tell me how you would choose palettes and plot structure so a scientific graphic stays readable and accessible.

title: Heatmaps and clustering visualization
summary: Build clustered heatmaps with explicit normalization, annotation, and distance or linkage assumptions.
test_prompts: Plan a clustered heatmap and explain what normalization and annotation choices must be explicit before interpretation.
Summarize the main reasons a heatmap can look convincing while still being analytically misleading.

title: Multipanel figure assembly
summary: Assemble multiple plots into consistent scientific figures with controlled layout, legend, and label behavior.
test_prompts: Explain how you would combine multiple plots into one figure without hiding inconsistent scales or styling.
Tell me what checks are needed before exporting a multi-panel scientific figure.

title: Circular genome plots
summary: Create circular genome plots only when the structure of the data justifies a circos-style view.
test_prompts: Plan a circular genome plot and explain what track structure makes the circos view worthwhile.
Summarize the main mistakes that make circular genome figures unreadable or misleading.

title: Figure export and delivery
summary: Export scientific figures with correct sizing, format, resolution, and typography for the target deliverable.
test_prompts: Explain how you would export a scientific figure for publication without clipping, font, or resolution surprises.
Tell me what format and sizing decisions matter most when a figure must work across papers and presentations.

title: Analysis reporting workflow
summary: Coordinate QC aggregation, result summarization, figure creation, and reproducible report packaging into one reporting workflow.
test_prompts: Outline an analysis-reporting workflow that starts from finished results and ends with export-ready figures and reproducible documents.
Adapt a reporting workflow for an omics project that needs QC aggregation, DE summary, heatmaps, and final figure export.

title: Molecular descriptors and fingerprints
summary: Compute molecular descriptors and fingerprints from standardized compounds for screening, ranking, or modeling tasks.
test_prompts: Explain how you would standardize compounds before computing descriptors or fingerprints for downstream use.
Tell me what descriptor choices matter most when the output will feed chemical screening or machine learning.

title: Molecular similarity searching
summary: Search compound libraries by molecular similarity with explicit representation choice and hit-threshold logic.
test_prompts: Plan a molecular similarity search and explain how fingerprint choice changes the meaning of the hits.
Summarize what you would report so analog-search results remain reproducible and interpretable.

title: Molecular substructure search
summary: Search compound collections for SMARTS and scaffold matches with explicit query-pattern assumptions.
test_prompts: Explain how you would run a substructure search without making the SMARTS query too broad or too brittle.
Tell me what validation checks matter before trusting scaffold-level filtering results.

title: Chemoinformatics screening workflow
summary: Coordinate descriptor generation, similarity search, and substructure filtering into one reproducible chemoinformatics workflow.
test_prompts: Outline a chemoinformatics workflow that standardizes molecules, computes features, and then performs similarity and substructure screening.
Adapt a compound-screening workflow for a project that starts from SMILES and ends with a shortlist of interpretable hits.
